title: Recover better
copy: On days when your training intensity reached [X], your next-day HRV tended to drop below [Y] ms.
how_it_works: The downward direction of the regression line suggests that harder sessions are currently creating more recovery stress, making post-training recovery especially important for protecting next-day HRV.

title: Reduce Sleep Disruptions
copy: When sleep disruptions exceeded [X] per hour, next-day HRV typically fell below [Y] ms.
how_it_works: The downward direction of the regression line suggests that more sleep disruptions are linked to lower next-day HRV, making sleep continuity especially important for protecting overnight recovery.

title: Improve Deep Sleep
copy: On nights when your Deep Sleep dropped below [X] min, your next-day HRV tended to fall below [Y] ms.
how_it_works: The upward direction of the regression line suggests that higher HRV is linked to more deep sleep, making deep sleep quality especially important for recovery.

title: Improve REM Sleep
copy: On nights when your REM Sleep dropped below [X] min, your next-day HRV tended to fall below [Y] ms.
how_it_works: The upward direction of the regression line suggests that higher HRV is linked to more rem sleep, making rem sleep quality especially important for recovery.

title: Improve Recovery Quality
copy: When your 7-day average training intensity stayed above [X], your 7-day average HRV tended to remain below [Y] ms.
how_it_works: This graph suggests that higher training intensity across the week was usually associated with lower baseline HRV, making sustained recovery quality especially important for protecting long-term HRV.

title: Reduce Overnight Awake Time
copy: On nights when your awake time rose above [X] minutes, your next-day HRV tended to drop below [Y] ms.

title: Reduce HRV Carryover
copy: When your HRV dropped below [X] ms, it often remained constrained the next morning instead of fully rebounding toward baseline.
how_it_works: The upward direction of the regression line suggests that low HRV tends to carry into the next morning, making recovery especially important for a rebound to baseline.
